description:
# Why

bl-a7d9 closed the architectural side of \"master wins\" for plugins, but the maintainer workflow for actually publishing plugin config on the hub is hand-editing `.balls/state-repo/.balls/{config.json,plugins/*.json}` followed by `git add/commit/push` on `balls/tasks`. Workable, but unergonomic — especially for a bare-only hub which has no obvious \"edit here\" spot; the maintainer has to operate through some participant clone.

This isn't only a master_url concern. Even on standalone repos, plugin config today is hand-edited JSON with no validation feedback until the next `bl` command tries to dispatch. A first-class `bl plugin` surface is cleaner in both modes.

# Concrete deliverable

- `bl plugin enable <name> [--config-file <path>] [--sync-on-change]` — adds an entry to the effective plugins map and stages the per-plugin config file. Honors the master-wins seam (bl-a7d9): in master_url mode the writes land in the state-repo's checkout on `balls/tasks`; in standalone mode they land on the project's main.
- `bl plugin disable <name>` — removes the entry. Per-plugin config file kept (operators may keep credentials warm).
- `bl plugin list` — shows the effective map and where each entry came from (project vs hub).
- Schema/validation: reject unknown participant policies (mirrors `validate_drop_policies`) at the command, not just at load.

# Out of scope

- Per-task plugin overrides (bl-a7d9 punted; separate ball if it grows legs).
- A web UI / GUI.
- A bare-only hub still has no working tree; the command runs from any participant clone whose state-repo materializes the hub. Same shape as task-editing today (you don't `bl create` directly on a bare hub either).

# Origin

bl-a7d9 close-out (2026-05-20). Notes during the post-merge review:
\"Hub-side plugin management has no tooling. Maintainers must hand-edit ... and `git push`. Workable but unergonomic, especially for a bare hub.\"
title: bl plugin enable/disable/list — hub-aware plugin management
